title: 帷幕
summary:
Following an inquest into the death of Mrs. Franklin, the coroner concludes that she died of curare poisoning. Based on witness testimony regarding her severe depression and Hercule Poirot’s claim that he saw her carrying a small bottle from the laboratory, the jury delivers a verdict of suicide due to mental abnormality.

Hastings later confronts Poirot in his room, questioning the truth of his testimony. Poirot admits that while he did not lie, he intentionally shaped his statements to ensure a suicide ruling. He reveals to Hastings that he believes Mrs. Franklin was actually the victim of a premeditated murder. Poirot intends to continue investigating the case secretly to catch the killer, whom he refers to as "X."

Following the funeral at Styles, Hastings is approached by an old lady who suggests that a similar murder occurred at Styles Manor twenty years prior, involving a wife whom everyone believed was treated well by her husband. This encounter leaves Hastings unsettled by the suspicion cast upon him.

Later, Hastings learns from Curtis that Poirot has suffered a severe heart attack. Unlike his usual behavior of seeking immediate medical attention for minor ailments, Poirot refuses to see more doctors, claiming he has already consulted specialists including Dr. Branco, Dr. Dash, and a cardiologist referred to as "R." Poirot expresses a fatalistic view regarding his declining health, noting that a body cannot be repaired like a machine.
